# TETA+PI — Verification Layer for the Agent Internet
# https://tetapi.dev
# TetaPi GmbH · Frankfurt am Main, Germany · 2026

## What is TETA+PI?

TETA+PI is a cryptographic trust protocol and verification registry for the Agent Internet.
Businesses, journalists, artists, and other entities get verified through official registries,
C2PA content signing, and Bitcoin timestamping — then become discoverable by AI agents via MCP.

## Protocol Stack (θ+π)

- Layer 1 — Registry: Cross-reference against government and industry registries (Handelsregister, GLEIF, EU VAT, DE-ÖKO, DJV, C2PA)
- Layer 2 — C2PA: Verified entities receive a cryptographic signing key for content authenticity
- Layer 3 — Bitcoin: Each verification record is timestamped on the Bitcoin blockchain (OpenTimestamps)
- Transport — MCP: AI agents access all three layers via the Model Context Protocol

## For AI Agents

MCP endpoint: https://mcp.tetapi.dev
API endpoint: https://api.tetapi.dev/api/v1
Agent manifest: https://app.tetapi.dev/.well-known/agent.json
API docs: https://api.tetapi.dev/docs

### Available MCP tools
- teta_search — search verified entities by name, domain, or registry ID
- teta_verify_entity — verify a specific entity and return trust level
- teta_verify_endpoint — verify that a domain/endpoint belongs to a verified entity
- teta_get_proof — retrieve cryptographic proof (C2PA + BTC timestamp) for an entity

### Trust levels returned
- registry_attested — entity confirmed in official registry
- c2pa_verified — entity has active C2PA signing credentials
- btc_confirmed — verification record anchored to Bitcoin blockchain

## Site pages

- / — Landing page, overview of the protocol
- /about.html — About TetaPi GmbH, mission, values
- /for-agents.html — MCP integration guide for AI agents (this page)
- /for-businesses.html — How businesses get verified
- /how-it-works.html — Technical explanation of the three-layer protocol
- /registries.html — Full list of supported verification registries
- /privacy.html — GDPR privacy policy
- /terms.html — Terms of service
